title: Centre for Energy and Materials - Home
gist: The World Economic Forum's Centre for Energy and Materials convenes 300+ business partners and 450 global leaders across 10 initiatives to accelerate a secure, sustainable, and equitable energy and materials transition, with workstreams spanning power systems, industrial cluster decarbonization, and critical mineral supply chain resilience.

insights.claim: The World Economic Forum's Centre for Energy and Materials aims to accelerate the transition to a secure, sustainable, and equitable energy system that enables positive economic and social development.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Signals the WEF's framing of the transition as a public-private collaboration agenda investors and policymakers may align around.
insights.quote: We strive to accelerate the transition to a secure, sustainable, and equitable energy system that enables positive economic and social development.
insights.locator: Our purpose is to bring together leaders

insights.claim: The Centre for Energy and Materials engages over 300 business partners and 450 global leaders across 10 global initiatives.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Indicates the scale of corporate buy-in shaping transition standards and coalitions.

insights.claim: The Securing Minerals for Energy and Technologies initiative advances collaboration to strengthen the resilience of minerals, promote responsible development, and mobilize finance for global mineral value chains.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
entities: Securing Minerals for Energy and Technologies
insights.so_what: Critical mineral supply security is a named priority, relevant to miners, EV, and clean-tech supply chains.
insights.quote: Advances collaboration to strengthen the resilience of minerals, promote responsible development and mobilize finance for global mineral value chains.
insights.locator: Securing Minerals for Energy and Technologies

insights.claim: The Future of Power Systems initiative seeks to accelerate power system transformation through innovation and finance to meet growing energy demand while advancing affordability, sustainability, and energy security.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Rising energy demand plus the affordability-security-sustainability trilemma frames where transition capital is being steered.
insights.quote: Accelerating power system transformation through innovation and finance to meet growing energy demand

insights.claim: The Transitioning Industrial Clusters initiative supports collaboration among co-located companies and public institutions to drive economic growth and employment while reducing CO2 emissions.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Cluster-based decarbonization is a recurring policy model worth tracking for industrial and infrastructure exposure.
insights.quote: with the goals of driving economic growth, employment and reducing CO2 emissions

top_entities: Securing Minerals for Energy and Technologies
